headline: Musk Applauds NFL Star Bosa For Paying Fine Over MAGA Hat
summary: San Francisco 49ers player Nick Bosa was fined for sporting a "Make America Great Again" hat during a post-game interview, winning praise from Elon Musk.

headline: Nvidia's Huang Sees AI Employees On The Horizon
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang anticipates that the corporate world will soon see "AI employees" as a standard part of the workforce.

headline: Mark Cuban, Tim Cook Reveal Life-Changing Email Hack To Save Time
summary: In a bid to tackle the challenge of overflowing inboxes, Mark Cuban and Tim Cook have turned to AI tools.

headline: Bill Gates Ready To Give Up 62% Of His Fortune, Advocates For Wealth Tax
summary: Bill Gates recently expressed his readiness to relinquish 62% of his wealth under a proposed tax system aimed at the affluent.

headline: Amazon's AI Investment, Palantir's Winning Strategy, Meta's Bee Problem, And Palantir's 'Eye-Popping' Results: This Week In AI
summary: Amazon may invest in Anthropic again, Palantir's unique AI strategy, Meta's plans for nuclear-powered data center stung by rare bees, and Super Micro Computer's governance issues and Q1 earnings.

headline: New Head Strap For Apple Vision Pro, Election Tracking, AI Magic, And More: This Week In Appleverse
summary: Apple's busy week: Vision Pro gets new head strap, Dynamic Island transformed for election tracking, iOS 18.2 beta brings AI features, Control Center customization, and bridging iOS-Android messaging gap.

headline: Tesla Faces NHTSA Scrutiny Over Social Media Promotion Of FSD Technology That Shows 'Disengaged Driver Behavior'
summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) flagged concerns about Tesla Inc's projection of its Full Self Driving (FSD) driver assistance software on social media platform X in a letter to the EV giant.

headline: Warren Buffett's Apple Stake Reduction Spurs $97B Cash Surge—Analysts Speculate This Could Be The Reason Behind Berkshire Hathaway's Massive Cash Pile
summary: Warren Buffett, the legendary investor, has made a significant move by reducing his stake in Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) and other stocks, generating a whopping $97 billion in gains for his company, Berkshire Hathaway Inc. (NYSE: BRK-A) (NYSE: BRK-B).
